hero-lede: You keep hearing the word agent everywhere, and it sounds technical and a little scary. It isn't. On this page you don't read about agents — you assemble one, piece by piece, with no code and no jargon, and see what each piece changes. Drag, tap, and see for yourself.

s1-p1: An agent is simply an AI you've handed some standing instructions to. You write down, just once, who it should be and how it ought to help — and from then on it behaves that way every time, for anyone who comes to talk to it. A little worker you describe using nothing but words.

An agent is just an AI you've given standing instructions to. You write down, once, who it should be and how it should help — and from then on it behaves that way every time, for anyone who talks to it. A little worker you describe in words.

An agent is an AI you've given standing instructions to. You write down, once, who it should be and how it should help — and from then on it behaves that way every time, for anyone who talks to it. A little worker you describe in words.
An agent is an AI you've given standing instructions: write down once who it is and how it helps, and it behaves that way every time, for anyone. A little worker you describe in words.
An agent: an AI with standing instructions — written once, applied every time, for anyone. A worker you describe in words.
s1-p2: If you can describe a job to a brand-new colleague in plain everyday English, you can write one. Below, you'll assemble the actual instructions, line by line, and see what each one changes. Here's a question worth holding onto as you go: when a reply comes back bad, is that the AI's fault — or the instructions'?

s2-p1: The AI below is the very same one every time — nothing about it changes. The only thing that changes is what you tell it. Start vague, then add one piece at a time, and read what comes back. s3-p1: That birthday request was a one-off — you asked it once and you got back a single answer. An agent is exactly the same trick, except it's written down just once and pointed at a whole job instead of a single question — and it ends up talking to total strangers, not only to you. Because of that, it needs a couple of extra pieces: the facts it has to stick to, and the limits on what it's actually allowed to promise. Let's go ahead and build a real one.

That birthday request was a one-off — you asked once, you got an answer. So it needs a couple more pieces: the facts it must stick to, and the limits of what it's allowed to promise. Let's build a real one.

That birthday request was a one-off: asked once, answered once. An agent is the same trick written down once and aimed at a job, talking to strangers, not just you. So it needs two more pieces: the facts it must stick to and the limits on what it can promise. Let's build one.
A one-off: asked once, answered once. An agent is that, written once, aimed at a job, for strangers — so add the facts it follows and the limits it can promise. Build one.
s4-p1: Say hello to a shoe shop that wants an agent to handle all its refund questions. Down below, it starts with no instructions at all — so when a real customer writes in, watch closely what it does. Then add each instruction, one by one, and watch what each one changes.

s5-recap:
• Who it is — the role you give it to play.
  • Its job — what it helps people with (and what it should leave well alone).
  • The facts it has to follow — your policy, your prices, your truth.
  • Its limits — the things it must never promise or do.
  • When it isn't sure — say so plainly, and pass it to a person.
  • Its tone — warm, plain, and brief.
  • The answer shape — what the reply should look like.

  • When it isn't sure — say so, and hand off to a person.
  • Its tone — warm, plain, brief.
  • The answer shape — how the reply should look.

s5-honest: An honest note: every reply above is a representative example rather than a live call to a model — so you can learn the move without all the noise getting in the way. Want the real thing, surprises and all? Take any version into chatty and run it on a live model.
